_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: BOT (Under supervision of Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation specialist)
clinical_indications: Multiple disability requiring rehabilitation, as evidenced by past history and diagnostic reports.
Disabilities stemming from conditions such as Cerebrovascular Accident (CVA), Cerebral Palsy, or Brain Injury, as confirmed by CT/MRI Brain reports.
Intellectual or learning disability, as confirmed by Electroencephalogram (EEG) reports.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
Details past history with treatment records
Electroencephalogram (EEG) report in intellectual and learning disability
CT/MRI Brain report in CVA/Cerebral palsy/Brain injury/intellectual/learning disability

claim.label: Indoor case papers with treatment given details

ppd_preauth.q: Does past history with treatment records of patient & diagnostic report suggestive of rehabilitation treatment?

common_queries: Past history or diagnostic reports do not suggest the necessity of rehabilitation treatment.
EEG report for intellectual or learning disability is missing or inconclusive when indicated.
CT/MRI Brain report for conditions like CVA, cerebral palsy, or brain injury is missing or inconclusive when indicated.
Clinical notes, evaluation findings, or planned line of treatment are missing or incomplete.
Detailed patient past history or treatment records are missing.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB (General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine, Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation), DM/DNB (Neurology)
clinical_key_pointers: Rehabilitation is defined as “a set of interventions designed to optimize functioning and reduce disability in individuals with health conditions in interaction with their environment. Rehabilitation is an essential part of universal health coverage along with promotion of good health, prevention of disease, treatment and palliative care. Rehabilitation helps a child, adult or older person to be as independent as possible in everyday activities and enables participation in education, work, recreation and meaningful life roles such as taking care of family.
